humanSummary.text: Iter-8 correctness audit of KAIFUU-011 (Binary patcher composed smoke) at commit bc8a3cd on spec/kaifuu-011. The slice ships `crates/kaifuu-cli/src/binary_patch_smoke.rs` (580 LOC) + the CLI dispatch arm in `main.rs:204-230` + `crates/kaifuu-cli/tests/binary_patch_smoke.rs` (189 LOC, 6 integration cases) + `packages/localization-bridge-schema/test/binary-patch-smoke.test.ts` (159 LOC, 5 TS validator cases). Total 14 tests as planned (6 integration + 3 unit + 5 TS validator) — all green: `cargo test -p kaifuu-cli` reports `81 passed` (lib + unit) and `9 passed` (the integration suite that includes the 6 smoke cases). The composition is structurally complete and exercises real bytes: (1) KAIFUU-174 `apply_patches` is invoked at line 293 against a real `build_synthetic_seen_txt` archive (47 bytes, single SEEN.TXT scene with one 6-byte dialogue slot) plus a real `build_synthetic_slot_edit`; (2) KAIFUU-084 `PatchTransaction` is constructed at line 367 with a full `PatchTransactionConfig` (adapter_id, patch_export_id, bridge_unit_id, expected_source_hash, expected_output_hash, expected_payload_len, byte_budget, required_transforms, adapter_capabilities) and driven through the full state machine `run_preflight() -> stage() -> verify() -> promote()` (lines 369-378) with an `is_terminal()` helper that names every terminal state (Promoted, PreflightFailed, VerifyFailed, PromoteFailed, Cancelled); (3) KAIFUU-010 PatchResult v0.2 is emitted from `outcome.patch_result_v02` (line 385) and immediately re-validated through `kaifuu_core::contracts::validate_patch_result_v02` (line 393) as defense in depth, plus the TS-side `assertPatchResultV02` walks the same JSON in `packages/localization-bridge-schema/test/binary-patch-smoke.test.ts`. The PatchBackErrorCode -> PatchFailureCategoryV02 mapping is materialised as `map_patchback_error_to_v02_failure` (lines 157-200) with 7 variant arms (OffsetOverflow, ShiftJisEncodeFailure, UnsupportedLengthPolicy, ParserRegression, UnknownSlotId, StaleSourceHash, ProtectedSpanLost) and an exhaustive-match unit test `map_patchback_error_to_v02_failure_is_exhaustive_over_every_variant` (line 526) that compile-errors if a new variant lands without a mapping. Three failure modes are exercised end-to-end: PreflightByteBudget (line 339-342, forces byte_budget=1 -> preflight rejection -> patch_write_failed); PreflightSourceHash (line 283-291, poisons SlotEdit.expected_source_hash -> StaleSourceHash -> source_incompatible via the patchback branch at line 293-313); VerifyHashMismatch (line 327-336, flips last byte after apply_patches -> VerifyFailed -> output_hash_mismatch and rollback). Cargo path-dep on kaifuu-reallive is added in `crates/kaifuu-cli/Cargo.toml`. F001 is a P2 follow-up: the `--inject-failure` flag is wired into main.rs:207 (`flag_optional(args, "--inject-failure")`) and dispatched via the unconditional `run_binary_patch_smoke_command` arm without ANY `#[cfg(...)]` or `#[cfg(feature = "...")]` gating. `grep -n 'cfg' crates/kaifuu-cli/src/main.rs` shows only test-context `#[cfg(test)]` and `#[cfg(unix)]` arms; no feature-flag gate covers the failure-injection seam. The audit-focus posture explicitly asks for `--inject-failure` to be cfg/feature-gated so a release-mode CLI cannot be coerced into a failure-shape emit by an end user. The current implementation lets any caller pass `--inject-failure preflight-byte-budget` against a real fixture and produce a Failed PatchResult — useful in CI, defensive when used by a release operator. The fix is to gate the `inject_failure` field of `BinaryPatchSmokeConfig` plus the CLI flag parse behind a `cfg(any(debug_assertions, feature = "failure-injection"))` attribute or equivalent, so production binaries refuse the flag. Verification: `cargo test -p kaifuu-cli` reports 81 + 9 passed across the lib and integration tests; `just check` exit 0 (fmt + clippy + ts:typecheck + schema:check clean). Outcome: `follow_up_only` — one P2 follow-up, no blocker. The composition, transaction-state coverage, mapping table exhaustiveness, and dual-validator posture are all sound.

findings.title: --inject-failure flag is not cfg/feature-gated; release CLI exposes failure-injection surface
findings.description: The `--inject-failure` CLI flag (parsed at main.rs:207-209) and the `BinaryPatchSmokeConfig.inject_failure` field (binary_patch_smoke.rs:71) are wired into the binary unconditionally. `grep -n 'cfg(' crates/kaifuu-cli/src/main.rs crates/kaifuu-cli/src/binary_patch_smoke.rs | grep -v '#\[cfg(test)\]'` returns only `#[cfg(unix)]` arms (unrelated) and the `#[cfg(test)]` unit-test module — no `#[cfg(any(debug_assertions, feature = ...))]` covers the failure-injection seam. A release-mode caller can run `kaifuu binary-patch-smoke --fixture <dir> --output <dir> --inject-failure verify-hash-mismatch --run-id <id>` and the binary will dutifully flip the last byte after `apply_patches` and emit a Failed PatchResult with `output_hash_mismatch` plus a rollback record. The audit-focus posture is explicit: failure-injection is a CI / development seam and the release CLI must not expose it (otherwise a downstream pipeline could be coerced into emitting Failed PatchResults that look authentic). The structural fix is straightforward: wrap the `inject_failure` field of `BinaryPatchSmokeConfig`, the `InjectFailure::parse` call, and the entire `--inject-failure` flag-parse arm in main.rs behind `#[cfg(any(debug_assertions, feature = "failure-injection"))]`, with a release-mode fallback that hard-codes `InjectFailure::None` and rejects the flag with an `unknown flag` error. Add a release-build test that asserts the flag is rejected and a debug-build test that asserts the three failure modes still work.

evidence.detail: `grep -n 'inject-failure\|inject_failure\|cfg(' crates/kaifuu-cli/src/main.rs crates/kaifuu-cli/src/binary_patch_smoke.rs | grep -v '#\[cfg(test)\]\|#\[cfg(unix)\]'` shows the `inject_failure` field and `--inject-failure` flag-parse arm with zero feature-gate or debug-assertion attributes.
main.rs:207-209 unconditionally parses `--inject-failure` via `flag_optional(args, "--inject-failure").unwrap_or("none")`; the result feeds straight into `BinaryPatchSmokeConfig` at lines 213-217.
binary_patch_smoke.rs:71 declares `pub inject_failure: InjectFailure,` as a public field of `BinaryPatchSmokeConfig` with no cfg gating.

The `InjectFailure` enum (preceding lines 60-71 area) and the parse helper are exposed as part of the public surface; no feature flag in `crates/kaifuu-cli/Cargo.toml` ties them to a release-time toggle.

findings.impact: A release-mode CLI can be coerced into producing Failed PatchResult JSON that looks authentic but is in fact a synthetic injection. Downstream pipelines that key off PatchResult shape (KAIFUU-010 validators on both Rust and TS sides happily accept the emitted JSON) cannot distinguish a real apply_patches failure from a forced failure-injection. Non-blocking because the smoke is well-documented and the flag's name makes intent obvious, but the audit-focus posture is that the seam should not exist in release binaries.
actionableAcceptanceCriteria: Add a `failure-injection` feature to `crates/kaifuu-cli/Cargo.toml` (default off in release, on in dev/test).

Wrap the `inject_failure` field of `BinaryPatchSmokeConfig` and the `InjectFailure::parse` import in `#[cfg(any(debug_assertions, feature = "failure-injection"))]`; in the cfg-off branch, the field is hard-coded to `InjectFailure::None`.
Wrap the `--inject-failure` flag-parse arm in main.rs in the same cfg attribute; the cfg-off branch returns an `unknown flag` error when the flag is supplied.

Add one integration test that builds with `--release --no-default-features` and confirms `kaifuu binary-patch-smoke --inject-failure verify-hash-mismatch` exits non-zero with an `unknown flag` diagnostic.
Keep the existing 14 dev-mode tests green by enabling the `failure-injection` feature in `[dev-dependencies.kaifuu-cli]` or via the workspace test-runner default.

proposedDagNode.title: Gate kaifuu binary-patch-smoke --inject-failure behind a cargo feature flag
proposedDagNode.summary: Gate the failure-injection seam (`--inject-failure` flag + `InjectFailure` enum + `BinaryPatchSmokeConfig.inject_failure` field) behind `cfg(any(debug_assertions, feature = "failure-injection"))` so a release-mode `kaifuu` binary refuses the flag with an `unknown flag` diagnostic. Keeps the 14 existing tests green (dev/test enables the feature implicitly) while removing the failure-injection surface from release operators' reach.
deliverables: `#[cfg(...)]` attributes on `BinaryPatchSmokeConfig.inject_failure`, the `InjectFailure` enum, and the `--inject-failure` flag-parse arm in main.rs.
One additive `--release --no-default-features` integration test asserting the flag is rejected in release builds.
Documentation note in the KAIFUU-011 doc / runtime README that failure injection is a dev/CI-only seam.
acceptanceCriteria: `cargo build -p kaifuu-cli --release --no-default-features` produces a binary that rejects `--inject-failure` with an `unknown flag` error.
`cargo test -p kaifuu-cli` (with the default dev features) keeps all 14 existing tests green.
`grep -n '#\[cfg(any(debug_assertions, feature = \"failure-injection\"))\]' crates/kaifuu-cli/src/binary_patch_smoke.rs crates/kaifuu-cli/src/main.rs` returns hits on all three gated sites.
auditFocus: Dev / CI builds keep failure-mode coverage (preflight-byte-budget, preflight-source-hash, verify-hash-mismatch).
